samples.xml_description:
Design a traffic light with red, yellow, green, and pedestrian button. Reset signal (active low). Clock signal. Pedestrian button. Red output signal. Yellow output signal. Green output signal. idle s1_red s2_yellow s3_green idle s1_red s2_yellow s3_green

Implement a traffic light controller with a finite state machine (FSM) that controls the traffic light states based on the internal counter and the pedestrian button.

Define the states of the FSM as parameters in the parameter description section.

Define the internal counter and the state variable as registers in the logic description section.

Define the output signals as wires in the ports section.

Implement the state transition logic in the first always block.

Implement the counter logic in the second always block.

Implement the output logic in the third always block.

Use a case statement to implement the state transition logic.

Use a conditional operator to implement the counter logic.

Use a continuous assignment to implement the output logic.

Use the non-blocking assignment for all the registers.

Use the following template for the state transition logic:

always @(*) begin case (state) idle: begin if (pass_request && green) begin state = s1_red; end else begin state = idle; end end s1_red: begin if (cnt == 0) begin state = s3_green; end else begin state = s1_red; end end s2_yellow: begin if (cnt == 0) begin state = s1_red; end else begin state = s2_yellow; end end s3_green: begin if (cnt == 0) begin state = s2_yellow; end else begin state = s3_green; end end endcase end

Use the following template for the counter logic:

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin if (!rst_n) begin cnt <= 10; end else begin if (pass_request && green) begin cnt <= 10; end else if (!green && p_green) begin cnt <= 60; end else if (!yellow && p_yellow) begin cnt <= 5; end else if (!red && p_red) begin cnt <= 10; end else begin cnt <= cnt - 1; end end end

Use the following template for the output logic:

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin if (!rst_n) begin red <= 0; yellow <= 0; green <= 0; end else begin red <= p_red; yellow <= p_yellow; green <= p_green; end end
